_provenance.thesis: Jeswani, K.K. (2021). The Seismic Resilience of Critical Spatially-Distributed Building Portfolios. MASc Thesis, University of Toronto.
_provenance.methodology: Almufti & Willford (2013). REDi Rating System v1.0. Arup.
notes: All delay distributions are lognormal (theta=median in days, beta=log-standard deviation).
Units: days. YAML source uses days for inspection and weeks for all others; converted to days here (1 week = 7 days).

Default values (flat top-level fields) correspond to the PRIMARY SCHOOL ARCHETYPES group, Max Repair Class = 3 (the binding/conservative case). These serve as the unknown/fallback default so that scalar compute_recovery() and any current readers still work.

by_archetype: archetype-specific impeding factor groups. Each entry has financing, contractor, engineer sub-dicts with median_days and beta. Inspection (5 d/0.54) and long_lead (14 d/0.50) are the same for all archetypes.

PERMITTING vs ENGINEER split: Table 6-7 combines engineering mobilization + permitting as a single delay path. The split below (approx. 50/50 of combined median) is engineering judgment. LOW CONFIDENCE — flag for Kevin's review.

LONG-LEAD MATERIALS: No standalone theta/beta in Table 6-7. Glazing/curtain wall marked '(LL)' in Table D-16 but no days given. Value below adopted from REDi v1.0 general knowledge (14 days median, beta=0.50). LOW CONFIDENCE — verify against Almufti & Willford 2013 App. A4.3.
provenance_confidence: inspection=HIGH, financing=HIGH, contractor=HIGH, engineer_combined=HIGH, permitting=LOW (split is estimated), long_lead=LOW (no thesis value)

_inspection_note: Non-Essential Facility category. theta=5 days, beta=0.54. Source: Table 6-7 (p.135). Applies to all archetypes. provenance_confidence: HIGH.
_financing_note: SBA-Backed Loans category for primary school archetypes (default/fallback). theta=48 weeks * 7 = 336 days, beta=0.57. Source: Table 6-7 (p.135). provenance_confidence: HIGH.
_permitting_note: ENGINEERING JUDGMENT SPLIT. Table 6-7 gives combined 'engineer mobilization + permitting' as 4 weeks (28 days, beta=0.54) for Max Repair Class 3. Permitting estimated at ~50% of combined = 14 days; engineer_median_days takes remaining 14 days. LOW CONFIDENCE. provenance_confidence: LOW.
_contractor_note: Non-essential facility <20 stories, Max Repair Class = 3. theta=19 weeks * 7 = 133 days, beta=0.38. Source: Table 6-7 (p.135). provenance_confidence: HIGH. Alternate (Max RC=1): theta=7wks=49days, beta=0.60.
_engineer_note: ENGINEERING JUDGMENT SPLIT. Combined 'engineer mobilization + permitting' per Table 6-7 = 4 weeks (28 days, beta=0.54) for Max Repair Class 3. Engineer share estimated at ~50% = 14 days; permitting takes remaining 14 days. LOW CONFIDENCE. Alternate (Max RC=1): combined=2wks=14days/beta=0.32; (complete redesign): combined=42wks=294days/beta=0.45. provenance_confidence: LOW.
_long_lead_note: NO THESIS VALUE. Glazing/curtain wall components flagged as Long Lead (LL) in Table D-16 but no days or beta given. Value of 14 days median / beta=0.50 adopted from REDi v1.0 general knowledge. LOW CONFIDENCE — must verify against Almufti & Willford 2013 App. A4.3 or thesis Appendix D. provenance_confidence: LOW.

by_archetype._note: Archetype-specific impeding factor groups from Table 6-7 (p.135). Keys are canonical archetype IDs from ARCHETYPE_IDS. Inspection (5 d/0.54) and long_lead (14 d/0.50) are the same for all archetypes and are inherited from the flat top-level fields. Merged archetypes are resolved to their parent's group entry.

_provenance_gap_simple_wood.contractor: Table 6-7 lists contractor delay for simple/wood archetypes under 'max_repair_class_3' = 7 weeks (49 d, beta=0.35), but does NOT specify per-archetype Max RC dependency (RC1 vs RC3) for these non-engineered typologies. We apply the RC3 value (49 d) as the documented default. LOW CONFIDENCE — flag for Kevin's review.
_provenance_gap_simple_wood.engineer: Table 6-7 does NOT specify engineer mobilization for simple/wood archetypes (N-L, CHB-L, S3-L, W-L). Non-engineered buildings plausibly need no engineer mobilization. Assigned 0 d. LOW CONFIDENCE — flag for Kevin's review.

engineer._note: Engineer on contract (DPWH/City Engineering). 0 weeks. Source: Table 6-7 p.135. provenance_confidence: HIGH. beta=0.01 avoids log(0) in lognormal sampling.

_provenance.source: Jeswani (2021) MASc thesis, Table D-15 (Appendix D.7.1, p.312)
notes: Replacement-cost → financing-weeks curve from Table D-15 (REDi replacement-time delays).
Applied HERE as a replacement-cost → REPAIR FINANCING curve for SBA/primary-school buildings only.
This is a MEDIUM-confidence faithful extension approved by Kevin Jeswani 2026-06-30:
the Table D-15 curve was originally for replacement-time delay, not repair-financing delay.
For primary schools where repair costs span the same order-of-magnitude as replacement costs,
the curve is a reasonable proxy for cost-dependent SBA financing timelines.
Simple (Insurance 42 d) and wood (Private 105 d) financing remain FLAT — they are already
in low-cost brackets where the curve would not apply.
Units: replacement_cost_php in PHP_2020, financing_days = weeks * 7.
Interpolation: log-linear on replacement_cost_php, clamped at endpoints.
Beta (dispersion) = 0.57 (same as flat SBA beta, Table 6-7, HIGH confidence).

_sub_categories._note: Sub-category values for reference only; not loaded by ImpedingFactorParams. Superseded by by_archetype above.
